meaning: adj.无所不在的,同时遍在的
examples: These days the media are omnipresent.
The sound of sirens was an omnipresent background noise in New York.
We live with a value system that I call the Extrovert Ideal—the omnipresent belief that the ideal self is gregarious, and comfortable in the spotlight.
He is omnipresent on campus, attending everything from faculty awards events to dormitory pizza parties.
In effect, Hartz IV recipients put their lives in receivership to the omnipresent Jobs Center.
